content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Verified all three amendments against src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs and template_catalog.rs. Each identifies a real defect: (1) domain validation is under-enforcing the new bead contract while canonicalization silently backfills missing covered_by; (2) the milestone-bundle prompt never instructs the model to emit a stable bead_id used in depends_on/covered_by cross-refs; (3) validate_dependency_graph is masked by unrelated validate() errors because graph analysis is gated behind full-bundle validation passing. All three are accepted.

## Votes

- `fr-3-143a7e7b`: Accept
  Verified at bundle.rs:141-145 (only title is rejected when empty — no checks for description, bead_type, priority range 1..=3, or non-empty labels) and bundle.rs:1099-1107 (canonicalize_bundle backfills empty acceptance_map[].covered_by from derived coverage before the emptiness check at 289-293 runs against the canonical bundle, so a planner omitting bidirectional mapping passes). The contract exists only in prompt text; domain validation doesn't enforce it. Real defect in safety/correctness.
- `fr-3-387ce418`: Accept
  Confirmed at template_catalog.rs:337-349: the bullet list covers title, description, bead_type, priority, labels, depends_on, acceptance_criteria but never instructs the model to assign a stable bead_id. Meanwhile bundle.rs:1050-1065 only synthesizes implicit milestone-scoped 'bead-N' identifiers from workstream/bead slot order after parsing, so LLM-emitted depends_on / covered_by references have to guess hidden slot numbers. Without an explicit 'emit bead_id per bead and reuse it for cross-references' directive, cross-ref correctness depends on the model independently inferring slot numbering. Real prompt-contract defect.
- `fr-3-d31a05b7`: Accept
  Confirmed at bundle.rs:963-965 (validate_dependency_graph delegates to validated_canonical_bundle), bundle.rs:1041-1043 (validated_canonical_bundle calls bundle.validate()? first), and bundle.rs:273-325 (graph analysis inside validate() is gated by `if errors.is_empty()`). So any unrelated metadata, identity, or AC error short-circuits validate() before graph analysis runs and also causes validate_dependency_graph's ? to propagate those non-graph errors — graph diagnostics the helper is supposed to surface get suppressed on partially invalid bundles. Reasonable to decouple graph analysis from full-bundle validation or at minimum return graph findings alongside other errors.
